vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

requirements: CFRP box beam center fuselage
Avionics bay
Wing spar socket
Front GPS boom mount
Belly camera window

risk.desc: Potential thermal management issues due to high-altitude operation.
risk.mit: Implement active cooling systems in the avionics bay.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient structural integrity under design loads.
risk.mit: Conduct finite element analysis to validate structural integrity.

risk.desc: Electrical serviceability may be compromised due to complex routing.
risk.mit: Simplify electrical routing and ensure clear access paths.

risk.desc: Potential for thermal expansion mismatch between CFRP and aluminum components.

risk.desc: Possibility of service access being obstructed by internal components.

risk.desc: Potential for environmental damage due to exposure to rain, dust, and salt.
risk.mit: Apply protective coatings and seals to critical components.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to heat dissipation from motor and ESC.
risk.mit: Implement heat sinks and thermal management systems.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to complex interface requirements.
risk.mit: Develop detailed assembly jigs and alignment tools.

risk.mit: Review and adjust clearance arc dimensions.

risk.mit: Provide clear torque specifications and use torque wrenches.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

physics_paths.path: Rotor thrust loads are transferred from the rotor shaft through the tilt axis to the wing structure via the bearing seats.
Tilting motion is enabled by the 6309 bearing set and a servo-driven worm gear mechanism.
Heat generated by the motor and ESC is dissipated through the nacelle housing and into the surrounding air.
Servicing paths for the motor and ESC are provided through access covers on the nacelle housing.
Electrical connections for the motor and ESC are made through harnesses that run through the nacelle housing.

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: align wing spar socket to nacelle mount
align fuselage spine with nacelle mount
align servo linkage with tilt axis

manufacturing_strategy.datum: primary datum feature is the wing_spar_socket
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: tolerance stack-up for bolted interfaces is H7/h6
manufacturing_strategy.support: support structures are oriented to minimize material use and improve print quality
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: inspection focuses on dimensional accuracy and surface finish

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

physics_paths.description: Load from landing impacts and takeoffs transmitted through wheel axles to leg struts and then to the fuselage spine and wing spar sockets
Motion of the aircraft during landing and takeoff, with damping from spring-steel skid arms
Thermal dissipation from friction during landing and takeoff, primarily conducted through the skid arms and dissipated through the wheel hubs
Access for maintenance and replacement of the landing gear components, primarily through the service access cut
Pressure from the ground during landing, transmitted through the wheels and skid arms

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads
manufacturing_strategy.support: Orientation: vertical for FDM, horizontal for CNC
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual and tactile inspection for fit and finish, CMM for critical dimensions

verification_focus.description: Ensure the landing gear mass is within the specified budget
Set fixture points, thermal zones, and pressure faces for CFD/FEA analysis
Ensure proper assembly and disassembly sequence for field serviceability

risk.desc: Potential thermal dissipation issues due to limited airflow in the payload bay.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the inspection window and thermal duct to improve airflow.

risk.desc: Risk of cable strain due to insufficient strain relief geometry.
risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have strain relief features and routing follows minimum bend radius.

risk.mit: Design the service hatch to be removable without tools and ensure it is easily accessible.

risk.mit: Add keying or unique labels to all connectors to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Potential structural weakness in the nose bay flange.
risk.mit: Reinforce the nose bay flange with additional ribs or stiffeners.
